description: Known-answer vector for the Label 309 inclusion certificate (the informative companion that composes the normative rfc9162-sha256 list commitment, the RFC 9162 inclusion proof, and the COSE verifiable-data-structure encoding). It pins the byte-exact interoperability surface every conforming implementation MUST reproduce from the same inputs: the tree root, the bare IETF inclusion proof as a `bstr .cbor` byte string (the CBOR byte string whose contents encode the array [tree_size, leaf_index, inclusion_path], extractable and verifiable on its own), and the COSE / RFC 9162-aligned inclusion-certificate-proof CBOR map (which embeds that same `bstr .cbor`). The JSON certificate form is field/value-parity only (a serializer's whitespace and number/key formatting vary across languages) and is therefore not byte-pinned here; the CBOR proof is the byte-identical anchor. Inputs: a deterministic 4-leaf tree where leaf_i = SHA-256(single byte i); a fixed Cardano mainnet anchor; the certified target is the leaf at index 2 carrying the optional leaf_alg "sha2-256".
